text:
(1) The department of transportation, in collaboration with the departments of education and public safety and appropriate nonprofit organizations and advocacy groups, shall produce for the general public educational materials concerning legal requirements and safe practices for approaching controlled intersections as a pedestrian or while operating a low-speed conveyance, as defined in section 42-4-1412.5 (4)(b).

(2) The division of motor vehicles in the department shall include in updates to the “Colorado Driver Handbook” updated information regarding legal requirements and safe practices for approaching controlled intersections that reflects changes to prior law made by House Bill 22-1028 , enacted in 2022.
markdown:
## Title 42
#### Article 4. Regul. of Vehicles & Traffic
##### § 42-4-2501 — Educational materials

(1) The department of transportation, in collaboration with the departments of education and public safety and appropriate nonprofit organizations and advocacy groups, shall produce for the general public educational materials concerning legal requirements and safe practices for approaching controlled intersections as a pedestrian or while operating a low-speed conveyance, as defined in section 42-4-1412.5 (4)(b).

(2) The division of motor vehicles in the department shall include in updates to the “Colorado Driver Handbook” updated information regarding legal requirements and safe practices for approaching controlled intersections that reflects changes to prior law made by House Bill 22-1028 , enacted in 2022.

subsections.text: The department of transportation, in collaboration with the departments of education and public safety and appropriate nonprofit organizations and advocacy groups, shall produce for the general public educational materials concerning legal requirements and safe practices for approaching controlled intersections as a pedestrian or while operating a low-speed conveyance, as defined in section 42-4-1412.5 (4)(b).

The division of motor vehicles in the department shall include in updates to the “Colorado Driver Handbook” updated information regarding legal requirements and safe practices for approaching controlled intersections that reflects changes to prior law made by House Bill 22-1028 , enacted in 2022.
